description_long: This is a weathered stone grave monument featuring a prominent upright Latin cross at the top, set against a rugged, rock-like backdrop. An angelic figure with detailed wings is sculpted in relief, appearing to lean against the cross in a mournful pose. The base of the monument is tiered, with rectangular and beveled stone layers, some of which show signs of chipping and erosion. Moss and discoloration are visible on the lower sections, indicating age and exposure to the elements. The monument is substantial in size and appears to be made primarily of carved stone, likely marble or limestone. No visible inscriptions or text are discernible on the monument from the provided views.
description: A large, weathered stone grave monument with a Latin cross and an angel in relief, set on a tiered base with signs of age and erosion.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cross, angel in profile, and the detailed, tiered stone base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angel's wings and the rugged stone texture behind the cross.
description_view_2: Rear view with the cross, the back of the angel, and more pronounced weathering on the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the back of the monument, chipped stone, and the layered base.
